opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My hands and feet feel like they're submerged in boiling oil, and I get so dizzy I can't walk.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over a day my burning limb pain became unbearable and I go faint on standing — I can't tolerate sheets and I'm on my own.

symptom_narrative.duration: a day, severe, on a long-standing background
symptom_narrative.character: burning 'boiling oil' pain in hands and feet, spreading up, with dizziness on standing
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: a flare of my long-standing burning nerve pain, plus dizziness upright
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: orthostatic dizziness and sweating changes; no limb going white/cold
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have idiopathic small fibre neuropathy and flare, but this is severe

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: idiopathic small fibre neuropathy (normal EMG previously)

family_history_as_reported.narrative: nothing specific that I know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I live alone, near-faint standing, and there are obstacles near the bed

communication_style.typical_message_style: describes burning pain and dizziness clearly, in obvious distress

objective_data_offered.value: around 125/80 lying and ~100/65 standing on my home cuff
my hands and feet are warm with normal colour, not white/cold; sheets are unbearable to touch
